title: Image viewer closes automatically when losing focus
author: Mug521
body:
### Problem Statement


When you go to **Image History** and double-click an image, it opens in the ShareX Image viewer. This view allows you to navigate through your history using the left and right arrow keys.

🤔 **The issue:** The image viewer window closes automatically the moment it loses focus (for example, when clicking anywhere outside of it, like on a second monitor). 

This behavior significantly reduces the tool's value. When working with multiple monitors, users often need to keep the image preview open on one screen to reference it while working on another.


#### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open **Image History**.
2. Double-click any image to open it in the full viewer (from here you can navigate with the left/right arrows, which is very handy actually 👍).
3. Click anywhere outside the image viewer window (e.g., on another application or a second monitor).
4. The image viewer closes immediately.


#### Expected Behavior

The image viewer should remain open when it loses focus. It should only close when the user explicitly interacts with it to do so (e.g., clicking directly on the image, clicking a "close" button, pressing `ESC`, etc.).


### Possible Solution


* Prevent the window from closing automatically when losing focus. Instead, **it should only close when the user clicks directly on the image itself** (which would be the most logical behavior, I think), rather than clicking anywhere outside of it (like a second monitor).

* **Alternative:** Add a toggle setting in ShareX (e.g., *"Close image viewer on focus loss"*) so users can choose their preferred behavior.

* **Possible Improvement:** Based on extensive daily use of the tool, it would be highly useful to open a context menu when right-clicking on the previewed image (currently, right-clicking shares the same behavior as left-clicking and just closes the image).

title: Bug: Cut does not function correctly with pixelate
author: tishalane
body: Issue: The area outside of the cutout region becomes pixelated after applying a cutout region that intersects a pixelated region. Pixelated is not "cutout". Instead, it seems to remain put. The boundaries outside of the cutout shift within the pixelated region. Steps to recreate: 1. Add pixelated region. 2. Use the cutout tool to remove a horizontal slice that overlaps the inner boundaries of the pixelated region.

title: Feature: "Copy region to clipboard" tool for the image editor
author: AaronMittelmeier
body:
I built a "Copy region" tool for the image editor (both the legacy WinForms editor and the modern Avalonia editor) and wanted to share it in case it's useful.  I use the copy region tool in snag it quite a bit when I'm rearranging screenshots for UI ideation and it's basically the only thing that they have that you don't.  

What it does: select a rectangular sub-region of the image being edited and copy just those pixels (WYSIWYG, including annotations) to the clipboard, with an optional "Region copied to clipboard. Paste now?" prompt that pastes the region back onto the canvas as a selected, movable element.

Pull requests are disabled here, so the work is on my fork:
- Code: https://github.com/AaronMittelmeier/ShareX/tree/improvements
- Diff vs develop: https://github.com/ShareX/ShareX/compare/develop...AaronMittelmeier:ShareX:improvements

Happy to discuss or adjust if you'd like to bring it in.

body:
Hi! Some feedback for the new Image Editor.

First, cannot help but notice that it starts a lot slower than the old editor. This creates friction.

Second, there is no way to add a simple background for text without adding a "speech balloon". Or I cannot find it. Please consider adding that as an obvious option for the `Text` tool. 

Thank you for your work!

title: Add Tesseract OCR fallback for languages unsupported by Windows OCR
author: mamohtweak-glitch
body:
Hello,

I am using the latest version of ShareX on Windows 11.

ShareX currently relies on Windows OCR languages. Unfortunately, Windows OCR does not provide support for all languages. For example, Ukrainian (uk-UA) is installed in Windows, but there is no corresponding Windows OCR package available.

As a result, Ukrainian users cannot use OCR in ShareX even though the language is fully supported by Tesseract OCR.

--
anguage.Basic~~~uk-UA~0.0.1.0 Installed
Language.Handwriting~~~uk-UA~0.0.1.0 Installed

No Language.OCR~~~uk-UA package exists.

--

Would it be possible to add an optional OCR fallback using Tesseract for languages that are not available through Windows OCR?

Possible benefits:
- Support for many additional languages.
- Better accessibility for non-English users.
- Offline OCR remains available.
- Users could choose between Windows OCR and Tesseract OCR.




Thank you for considering this feature.

title: Remeber window state BUG
author: AficionadoT
body:
The "Remember window state" option works perfectly in the legacy image editor, but it doesn't work in the new image editor.

I use a multi-monitor setup and I want the image editor to always open on my secondary monitor instead of the primary one. With the legacy editor this works as expected, but with the new editor the window always opens on the primary monitor, regardless of where it was last closed.
